subject: PC (program counter) guarantee behavior
old: PC was not guaranteed to be synced/consistent in all cases
new: PC is now guaranteed to be consistent in all cases via an architecture-agnostic mechanism
truth: PC is now guaranteed to be consistent in all execution cases, whereas previously it was not.
quote: Unicorn (finally!) offers consistent PC guarantee in all cases. Well, it might sound weird that why it was not. Generally QEMU is not designed to keep PC synced in all cases for performance and Unicorn once put necessary hacks but those hacks were too hard to maintain. Now we have architecture agnostic mechanism to offer the PC guarantee.

from_fact: pc was not guaranteed to be synced/consistent in all cases
pc is now guaranteed to be consistent in all cases via an architecture-agnostic mechanism

subject: QEMU version bump (upcoming 2.2.0)
truth: Version 2.2.0 will bump QEMU to 5.1.0 or higher, which may change execution semantics.
quote: Please aware that 2.2.0 would probably bump QEMU version to 5.1.0 or even higher so semantics could be changed.

from_fact: qemu version prior to 5.1.0
qemu version 5.1.0 or higher (planned for 2.2.0)

subject: size limit for memory read and write
old: memory read/write operations had a size limit
truth: Stop relying on any size limit for uc_mem_read/uc_mem_write; the size limit has been removed and operations that previously would have been rejected due to size may now succeed or behave differently.
quote: Remove the size limit for memory read and write (revamped)

from_fact: memory read/write operations had a size limit
uc_mem_read/uc_mem_write

subject: x86 default CPU model
truth: The x86 default CPU model is now UC_CPU_X86_HASWELL; previous default CPU model is no longer used.
quote: x86 default cpu model is changed to `UC_CPU_X86_HASWELL` and remove a few quirks.

from_fact: previous default cpu model
uc_cpu_x86_haswell

subject: x86 CPU quirks
truth: Several x86 CPU quirks have been removed.
quote: x86 default cpu model is changed to `UC_CPU_X86_HASWELL` and remove a few quirks.

from_fact: certain x86 cpu quirks existed

subject: return value for non-existing registers
new: prints a warning instead of returning UC_ERR_ARG
truth: Accessing non-existing registers no longer returns UC_ERR_ARG; instead a warning is printed.
quote: Revert previous break changes that return UC_ERR_ARG for non-existing registers. Now this prints a warning. We urge all users relying on this behavior to fix code as soon as possible.

from_fact: returned uc_err_arg for non-existing registers
prints a warning instead of returning uc_err_arg

subject: memory allocation behavior
old: Unicorn allocated 2GB memory for every instance
new: Unicorn no longer allocates 2GB memory upfront; memory is committed only once used, upper limit adjustable via uc_ctl
truth: Stop relying on 2GB pre-allocation per instance; memory is now committed on demand and the upper limit must be set via uc_ctl.
quote: Now Unicorn no longer allocates 2GB memory for every instance. The memory will be only committed once used and the upper limit can be adjusted with `uc_ctl`.

from_fact: unicorn allocated 2gb memory for every instance
unicorn no longer allocates 2gb memory upfront; memory is committed only once used, upper limit adjustable via uc_ctl

subject: armeb-softmmu and aarcheb-softmmu targets
truth: Stop using armeb-softmmu and aarcheb-softmmu targets; they have been removed.
quote: Remove armeb-softmmu and aarcheb-softmmu which are usermode targets.

from_fact: armeb-softmmu and aarcheb-softmmu (usermode targets)

subject: VEX.L setting
old: VEX.L could be set without explicit failure
new: Unicorn now fails explicitly when VEX.L is set
truth: Setting VEX.L now causes an explicit failure instead of silently ignoring or misbehaving.
quote: Fail explicitly when VEX.L is set.

from_fact: vex.l could be set without explicit failure
unicorn now fails explicitly when vex.l is set
vex.l

subject: Value collision between UC_MODE_ARMBE8 and UC_MODE_ARM926
old: UC_MODE_ARMBE8 and UC_MODE_ARM926 had colliding values
truth: UC_MODE_ARMBE8 and UC_MODE_ARM926 no longer have colliding values; code that relied on the collision (e.g. using one value to mean the other) will break.
quote: Fix the value collision between UC_MODE_ARMBE8 and UC_MODE_ARM926

from_fact: uc_mode_armbe8 and uc_mode_arm926 had colliding values
the value collision is fixed
e.g

subject: ARM big endian softmmu targets
truth: Stop using armeb-softmmu and aarch64eb-softmmu targets; they have been dropped.
quote: Correctly support ARM big endian and drops `armeb-softmmu` and `aarch64eb-softmmu`

from_fact: armeb-softmmu and aarch64eb-softmmu targets

subject: API
truth: Stop using uc_context_free(), use uc_free() instead.

from_fact: uc_context_free()
uc_free()

subject: virtual-to-physical address mapping
old: Unicorn v1 provided a 1:1 mapping of virtual address to physical address by default
new: Unicorn 2 no longer provides 1:1 virtual-to-physical mapping by default; must enable via uc_ctl_tlb_mode(UC_TLB_VIRTUAL)
truth: Stop relying on default 1:1 virtual-to-physical address mapping; enable virtual TLB mode with uc_ctl_tlb_mode(UC_TLB_VIRTUAL) to restore it.
quote: Unicorn v1 previously hacks QEMU internal implementation to provide a 1:1 mapping of virtual address to physical address, which is super convenient in most cases.

from_fact: unicorn v1 provided a 1:1 mapping of virtual address to physical address by default
unicorn 2 no longer provides 1:1 virtual-to-physical mapping by default; must enable via uc_ctl_tlb_mode(uc_tlb_virtual)

subject: uc_emu_start error handling for memory hooks
old: uc_emu_start did not return an error if an error within memory hooks was not handled
new: uc_emu_start now returns an error if any error within memory hooks is not handled
truth: Stop ignoring unhandled errors in memory hooks; uc_emu_start will now return an error if memory hook errors are not handled, so you must correctly map regions.
quote: Also we made uc_emu_start return an error if any error within memory hooks is not handled. This is easy to migrate by correctly mapping the regions.

from_fact: uc_emu_start did not return an error if an error within memory hooks was not handled
uc_emu_start now returns an error if any error within memory hooks is not handled

subject: stopping within MIPS delay slots and ARM IT blocks
old: Unicorn allowed stopping within MIPS delay slots and ARM IT blocks, losing branch information
new: Unicorn 2 makes execution unstoppable within MIPS delay slots and ARM IT blocks to preserve correct semantics
truth: Stop expecting to pause/stop emulation inside MIPS delay slots or ARM IT blocks; Unicorn 2 will not stop in those blocks, reducing tracing availability.
quote: In all previous versions, if Unicorn happens to stop within the IT blocks or delay slots, the branch information is lost, i.e. incorrect emulation result. In Unicorn 2, we make it unstoppable in such blocks to ensure the correct semantics, at a cost of less tracing availability.

from_fact: unicorn allowed stopping within mips delay slots and arm it blocks, losing branch information
unicorn 2 makes execution unstoppable within mips delay slots and arm it blocks to preserve correct semantics
pause/stop
